description: Build /case-studies section with 10 SEO/AEO-optimized case study pages, filterable index, JSON-LD schema, internal linking to pillar pages, and site navigation integration.

userStories.title: Case study data schema, types, and utility functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed CaseStudy interface and utility functions so all downstream pages can load and display case studies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/case-study.ts with CaseStudy interface: slug, title, category (union: 'data-collection' | 'annotation' | 'safety' | 'evaluation' | 'video' | 'robotics' | 'gaming' | 'fashion-ai'), teaser (string), headlineStat (string), headlineStatLabel (string), summary (string), challenge (string), approach (string), results (array of {value: string, label: string}), impact (string), whyItMatters (string), faqs (array of {question: string, answer: string}), relatedSlugs (string[]), pillarLink ({href: string, label: string}), targetKeyword (string), metaTitle (string), metaDescription (string), images (string[]), datePublished (ISO string)

Create src/lib/case-studies.ts with: getAllCaseStudies() returning CaseStudy[], getCaseStudyBySlug(slug: string) returning CaseStudy | null, getCaseStudiesByCategory(category: string) returning CaseStudy[], getRelatedCaseStudies(slug: string, limit?: number) returning CaseStudy[]
Functions read from src/data/case-studies/*.json files using fs/path (server-side only, same pattern as src/lib/jobs.ts)
Create src/data/case-studies/ directory
Add 1 sample JSON file for testing (egocentric-video-collection.json)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed all 10 case study JSON files from markdown docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all 10 case study JSON files populated with content so the pages have real data.
acceptanceCriteria: Read each markdown doc in docs/case-studies/*.md and create corresponding JSON file in src/data/case-studies/

All 10 files created: egocentric-video-collection.json, game-based-data-capture.json, fashion-ai-annotation.json, generative-ai-safety.json, video-model-evaluation.json, workplace-egocentric-data.json, object-identity-persistence.json, prompt-enhancement-benchmark.json, video-content-classification.json, data-engine-world-models.json

Each JSON includes expanded content: challenge (2-4 sentences, answer-first), approach (2-3 paragraphs with methodology detail using technical vocabulary), results (3-4 stat objects), impact paragraph, whyItMatters paragraph
Each JSON includes 3-5 FAQs with answer-first format — direct statement then elaboration
Content tone: written for skeptical AI researcher, NO marketing fluff, concrete methodology, technical terms (egocentric POV, semantic segmentation, Krippendorff alpha, ELO ranking)
Each JSON includes metaTitle (<60 chars) and metaDescription (<155 chars) targeting the primary keyword

Target keywords per case study: egocentric-video-collection targets 'egocentric video data collection robotics', game-based-data-capture targets 'game-based data collection AI training', fashion-ai-annotation targets 'fashion image annotation AI training data', generative-ai-safety targets 'generative AI safety labeling service', video-model-evaluation targets 'video generation model evaluation service', workplace-egocentric-data targets 'workplace egocentric data robotics training', object-identity-persistence targets 'object identity tracking video annotation AI', prompt-enhancement-benchmark targets 'prompt enhancement benchmarking training data', video-content-classification targets 'video content classification training data', data-engine-world-models targets 'data engine for world models AI'

Each JSON includes pillarLink mapping: egocentric-video -> /pillars/acquire/egocentric-video, game-based -> /pillars/acquire/synthetic-data, fashion-ai -> /pillars/enrich/expert-annotation, safety -> /pillars/validate/red-teaming, video-model-eval -> /pillars/validate/benchmark-curation, workplace -> /pillars/acquire/egocentric-video, object-identity -> /pillars/enrich/video-annotation, prompt-benchmark -> /pillars/validate/benchmark-curation, video-classification -> /pillars/enrich/expert-annotation, data-engine -> /pillars/acquire
Each JSON includes relatedSlugs array with 2-3 thematically adjacent case studies
Each JSON includes images array with placeholder paths like /images/case-studies/[slug]/hero.png (assets downloaded in US-003)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Download media assets from Notion case study pages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a script that downloads images from the Notion case study pages to public/images/case-studies/[slug]/ so pages can reference local assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/download-case-study-assets.ts runnable with npx tsx scripts/download-case-study-assets.ts
Script reads the Notion image URLs listed in each docs/case-studies/*.md file under the Notion Asset URLs section
For each case study, creates public/images/case-studies/[slug]/ directory
Downloads each image URL and saves with descriptive filename (hero.png, metrics.png, process.png, etc.)
Script handles expired Notion S3 URLs gracefully — logs warning but does not crash
Script outputs summary: X images downloaded for Y case studies
After running, update the images arrays in src/data/case-studies/*.json to reference the actual downloaded file paths
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Case studies index page with filterable card grid
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse all case studies on a filterable index page so I can find projects relevant to my needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/case-studies/page.tsx
Create src/app/case-studies/layout.tsx with metadata: title 'Case Studies | AI Training Data Projects at Scale | Claru', description targeting 'AI training data case studies'
Uses shared Header (with opaque prop) and Footer components
Section label: // CASE STUDIES in monospace accent-secondary
Headline: What we have shipped.
Subheadline: From egocentric video to model evaluation — real projects, real numbers.
Category filter pills: All, Data Collection, Annotation, Safety, Evaluation, Video, Robotics, Gaming — clicking filters the grid
Card grid: responsive 1-col mobile, 2-col tablet, 3-col desktop
Each card shows: category badge (colored by type), headline stat in large monospace, title, 2-line teaser, arrow link to /case-studies/[slug]
Cards use --bg-card background, --border-subtle border, hover lift and border glow matching existing site patterns
TextScramble on card title hover
whileInView staggered fade-in animations
Count display: Showing X case studies
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Individual case study page template with full SEO
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read a detailed case study page with methodology, results, and related content so I can evaluate Claru's capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/case-studies/[slug]/page.tsx with generateStaticParams for static generation

Page structure in order: (1) Hero with outcome-led headline + category badge + headline stat, (2) Quick Summary terminal-style callout block with challenge/solution/result in 3 lines, (3) Snapshot bar with 3-4 inline stats, (4) Challenge section (answer-first: direct statement then 2-4 sentences), (5) Our Approach section (2-3 paragraphs with methodology detail), (6) Results section (3-column stat grid with large monospace numbers in accent-primary), (7) Impact section (1 paragraph), (8) Service Used callout linking to the relevant pillar page with keyword-rich anchor text, (9) Related Case Studies 3-card grid using getRelatedCaseStudies, (10) FAQ accordion section with 3-5 Qs answer-first, (11) CTA section with Book a Call button
Article JSON-LD schema injected via script tag: headline, datePublished, author (Claru AI org), publisher, image, description
FAQPage JSON-LD schema injected for the FAQ section
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD schema: Home > Case Studies > [Title]
Open Graph meta tags: title, description, image, type article
Twitter card meta tags: summary_large_image
Dynamic generateMetadata using the case study metaTitle and metaDescription fields
Images from public/images/case-studies/[slug]/ displayed where relevant (hero image, process diagrams)
Responsive layout matching existing site dark terminal aesthetic
TextScramble on headline, FadeIn scroll animations on sections
404 handling: return notFound() if slug not found
npm run build succeeds — all 10 pages generate statically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Case Studies to site navigation
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see Case Studies in the header navigation so I can discover the section from any page.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/components/layout/Header.tsx add Case Studies link to navLinks array
Nav order: Training Data, Expert Labeling, Case Studies, Work With Us, [Book a Call CTA]
Link appears in both desktop nav and mobile menu
In src/app/components/sections/Footer.tsx add Case Studies link in the navigation section
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — check desktop and mobile nav

userStories.title: Add case study callout cards to pillar pages
userStories.description: As a visitor browsing a pillar page, I want to see relevant case studies so I can see proof that Claru has done this work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a reusable CaseStudyCallout component that takes a case study slug and renders: headline stat, title, 1-line teaser, Read case study arrow link
Component styled as a dark card with accent border, matching existing pillar page design

Add a See It In Practice section to these pillar pages with the mapped case study callout(s): /pillars/acquire/egocentric-video gets egocentric-video-collection + workplace-egocentric-data, /pillars/acquire/synthetic-data gets game-based-data-capture, /pillars/enrich/expert-annotation gets fashion-ai-annotation + video-content-classification, /pillars/enrich/video-annotation gets object-identity-persistence, /pillars/enrich/rlhf gets prompt-enhancement-benchmark, /pillars/validate/red-teaming gets generative-ai-safety, /pillars/validate/benchmark-curation gets video-model-evaluation + prompt-enhancement-benchmark
Section label on pillar pages: // SEE IT IN PRACTICE
Placed before the CTA section on each pillar page
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — check at least 3 pillar pages

userStories.title: Playwright E2E tests for case studies section
userStories.description: As QA, I need end-to-end tests verifying the case studies index, individual pages, structured data, and navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/case-studies.spec.ts
Test: navigate to /case-studies, verify 10 case study cards render
Test: click a category filter pill, verify cards filter correctly
Test: click a case study card, verify navigation to /case-studies/[slug]
Test: on individual page verify: headline, quick summary block, snapshot stats, challenge section, approach section, results grid, FAQ accordion, related case studies, CTA button
Test: verify Article JSON-LD exists in page source with required fields (headline, datePublished, author)
Test: verify FAQPage JSON-LD exists with at least 3 questions
Test: verify BreadcrumbList JSON-LD exists with 3 items
Test: verify breadcrumb navigation renders (Home > Case Studies > Title)
Test: verify Service Used callout links to a pillar page
Test: verify Case Studies link exists in header navigation
Test: check a pillar page has See It In Practice section with case study callout
Typecheck passes
